chemistry.reaction_name: Photochemical micro-flow-through polymerization with silver nanoparticle incorporation
chemistry.scope_summary: Synthesis of composite microparticles containing silver nanoparticles within a swellable polyacrylamide matrix for SERS sensing applications. Particles of ~100 μm diameter were produced with additional silver enhancement capability.

figures.caption: Figure 1.  (a) Lower- and (b) higher-magnification SEM images of the silver/polyacrylamide composite microparticles (sensor par- ticles) obtained after applying a 25 mM AgNO 3  solution to the surface. | Figure 2.  Scheme of the microflow arrangement for in situ SERS measurements using com- posite microgel sensor particles for interaction with different analytes in continuous flow.

figures.flow_path: Three syringe pumps (analyt solution, reference solution, reactivation solution) feed into measurement capillary containing SERS sensor particles, then to waste
figures.key_information: SERS-based flow analysis system with three input streams (analyt solution, reference solution with water, reactivation solution with diluted H2SO4) and inline Raman detection
figures.caption: Figure 1.  (a) Lower- and (b) higher-magnification SEM images of the silver/polyacrylamide composite microparticles (sensor par- ticles) obtained after applying a 25 mM AgNO 3  solution to the surface. | Figure 2.  Scheme of the microflow arrangement for in situ SERS measurements using com- posite microgel sensor particles for interaction with different analytes in continuous flow.

Figure 3.  SERS measurements in the compact SERS arrangement under micro- flow conditions using polyacrylamide/Ag composite microparticles inside a glass capillary (1) before and (2) during the application of an aqueous solution of histi- dine and (3) after rinsing with diluted sulfuric acid. | Figure 4.  Reproducibility of the SERS flow sensing: Individual SERS spectra dur- ing the flow of an adenine solution (40  m L min −1 ), obtained at a measurement frequency of 1 Hz. The baseline of the single spectra was shifted by 5 units be- tween the single measurements for better visibility.

Figure 3.  SERS measurements in the compact SERS arrangement under micro- flow conditions using polyacrylamide/Ag composite microparticles inside a glass capillary (1) before and (2) during the application of an aqueous solution of histi- dine and (3) after rinsing with diluted sulfuric acid. | Figure 4.  Reproducibility of the SERS flow sensing: Individual SERS spectra dur- ing the flow of an adenine solution (40  m L min −1 ), obtained at a measurement frequency of 1 Hz. The baseline of the single spectra was shifted by 5 units be- tween the single measurements for better visibility.

Figure 5.  Application and measurement of different analytes by repeated switching of the flows of the analyte and rinsing solutions. Flow rate: 40  m L min −1 ; applied concentra- tions: 10 mM histidine, 2 mM adenine, and 3 % sulfuric acid.

figure_descriptions: SERS-based flow analysis system with three input streams and inline Raman detection

conflicts.resolution: Used 0.04 mL/min as it matches 40 μL/min conversion

notes: This paper focuses on SERS sensing applications using flow-synthesized composite microparticles rather than traditional batch-to-flow reaction optimization. Limited quantitative reaction data available due to analytical chemistry focus. Reactor scheme provides good equipment details but optimization data is minimal.
